metadata.description: Friendly name of the workload profile.
Type of the workload profile.
Minimum number of nodes for the workload profile.
Maximum number of nodes for the workload profile.
Information about a workload profile for the environment.
Name of the Azure Developer CLI environment
The Azure region where your resources will be created.
The Azure region where Cosmos DB will be created. Defaults to the resource group location.
The Azure region where Azure AI Search services will be created. Defaults to the main deployment location. Override this when the primary region is out of capacity for AI Search.
Principal ID for role assignments. This is typically the Object ID of the user or service principal running the deployment.
Principal type for role assignments. This can be "User", "ServicePrincipal", or "Group".
Tags to apply to all resources in the deployment
Label used for App Configuration key-value pairs.
Enable network isolation for the deployment. This will restrict public access to resources and require private endpoints where applicable.

When set to true, Private DNS Zones and DNS zone groups will NOT be created by this module.
Use this option in environments where Azure Policy automatically manages Private DNS Zone linking for private endpoints
(e.g., CAF Enterprise-Scale Platform Landing Zone). Creating DNS zones in those environments causes conflicts with
policy-driven DNS management and results in deployment failures.
When false (default), the module creates and manages all Private DNS Zones and links them to the VNet.
Requires networkIsolation to be true to have any effect.
The Azure region where private endpoints will be created. Defaults to the main deployment location. Use this when your VNet is in a different region than the resources.

The name of the resource group where private endpoints will be created. When empty, private endpoints are placed in the VNet resource group (for existing VNets with sideBySideDeploy disabled) or the deployment resource group.
Use an existing Virtual Network. When false, a new VNet will be created.

The full ARM resource ID of an existing Virtual Network. Format: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnetName}. Leave empty to create a new VNet.
Address prefixes for the virtual network.
AI Foundry Agents subnet — Recommended /24 (256 IPs)
Azure Container Apps Environment subnet — /24 (256 IPs)
Private Endpoints subnet — /26 (64 IPs) — Increased to prevent race conditions during parallel PE creation
Azure Bastion subnet — Required /26 (64 IPs, CIDR-aligned)
Azure Firewall subnet — /26 (64 IPs, CIDR-aligned)
Gateway subnet — Required /26 (64 IPs, CIDR-aligned)
Application Gateway subnet — /27 (32 IPs)
Jumpbox subnet — /27 (32 IPs)
DevOps Build Agents subnet — /27 (32 IPs)
Whether to deploy Bing-powered grounding capabilities alongside your AI services.
Deploy Azure AI Foundry for building and managing AI models.
Deploy Azure AI Foundry agent subnet.
Deploy Azure App Configuration for centralized feature-flag and configuration management.
Deploy an Azure Key Vault to securely store secrets, keys, and certificates.
Deploy an Azure Key Vault to securely store VM secrets, keys, and certificates.
Deploy an Azure Log Analytics workspace for centralized log collection and query.
Deploy Azure Application Insights for application performance monitoring and diagnostics.
Deploy an Azure Cognitive Search service for indexing and querying content. When disabled, search-related connections are skipped and search app configuration values resolve to empty values.
Deploy an Azure Storage Account to hold blobs, queues, tables, and files.
Deploy an Azure Cosmos DB account for globally distributed NoSQL data storage.
Deploy Azure Container Apps for running your microservices in a serverless Kubernetes environment.
Deploy an Azure Container Registry to store and manage Docker container images.
Deploy the Container Apps environment (log ingestion, VNet integration, etc.).
Deploy a Virtual Machine (e.g., for jumpbox or specialized workloads).
Deploy the virtual network subnets.
Will deploy network security groups.
Deploy Azure Firewall with UDR for egress traffic control. Defaults to true when networkIsolation is enabled.
List of trusted source IP CIDRs allowed to connect to the Bastion public IP on port 443. When empty, all internet inbound to port 443 is denied by default.
Will deploy network resources side by side with the Azure resources.
Deploy Virtual Machine software.
Deploy AI Foundry Project.
Deploy AI Foundry Service.
Enable agentic retrieval features in Azure AI Search indexes. Requires vectorizer configuration and semantic search.
The AI Search Service full ARM resource ID. Optional; if not provided, a new resource will be created.
The AI Storage Account full ARM resource ID. Optional; if not provided, a new resource will be created.
The Cosmos DB account full ARM resource ID. Optional; if not provided, a new resource will be created.
The Key Vault full ARM resource ID. Optional; if not provided, a new vault will be created.
Unique token used to build deterministic resource names, derived from subscription ID, environment name, and location.
Name of the Azure AI Foundry account to create or reference.
Name of the AI Foundry project resource.
Name of the Storage Account used by AI Foundry for blobs, queues, tables, and files.
Name of the Cognitive Search service provisioned for AI Foundry.
Name of the Azure Cosmos DB account used by AI Foundry.
Name of the Bing Search resource for grounding capabilities.
Name of the Azure App Configuration store for centralized settings.
Name of the Application Insights instance for monitoring.
Name of the Azure Container Apps environment (log ingestion, VNet integration, etc.).
Name of the Azure Container Registry for storing Docker images.
Name of the Cosmos DB account (alias for database operations).
Name of the Cosmos DB database to host application data.
Name of the Azure Key Vault for secrets, keys, and certificates.
Name of the Log Analytics workspace for collecting and querying logs.
Name of the Cognitive Search service.
Name of the Azure Storage Account for general-purpose blob and file storage.
Name of the Virtual Network to isolate resources and enable private endpoints.
List of model deployments to create in the AI Foundry account
List of container apps to create
Workload profiles.
Optional throughput (RU/s) for the Cosmos DB database. Omit or set to null for serverless accounts.
List of Cosmos DB containers to create. Each entry supports optional throughput and indexingPolicy via safe access.
The name of the Test VM. If left empty, a random name will be generated.
Test vm user name. Needed only when choosing network isolation and create bastion option. If not you can leave it blank.
Admin password for the test VM user
Size of the test VM
Image SKU (e.g., win11-25h2-ent, win11-23h2-ent, 2022-datacenter).
Image publisher (Windows 11: MicrosoftWindowsDesktop, Windows Server: MicrosoftWindowsServer).
Image offer (Windows 11: windows-11, Windows Server: WindowsServer).
Image version (use latest unless you need a pinned build).
List of containers to create in the Storage Account

metadata.name: Virtual Networks
metadata.description: This module deploys a Virtual Network (vNet).
Optional. The Name of VNET Peering resource. If not provided, default value will be peer-localVnetName-remoteVnetName.
Required. The Resource ID of the VNet that is this Local VNet is being peered to. Should be in the format of a Resource ID.
Optional. Whether the forwarded traffic from the VMs in the local virtual network will be allowed/disallowed in remote virtual network. Default is true.
Optional. If gateway links can be used in remote virtual networking to link to this virtual network. Default is false.
Optional. Whether the VMs in the local virtual network space would be able to access the VMs in remote virtual network space. Default is true.
Optional. Do not verify the provisioning state of the remote gateway. Default is true.

Optional. If remote gateways can be used on this virtual network. If the flag is set to true, and allowGatewayTransit on remote peering is also true, virtual network will use gateways of remote virtual network for transit. Only one peering can have this flag set to true. This flag cannot be set if virtual network already has a gateway. Default is false.
Optional. Deploy the outbound and the inbound peering.
Optional. The name of the VNET Peering resource in the remove Virtual Network. If not provided, default value will be peer-remoteVnetName-localVnetName.
Optional. Whether the forwarded traffic from the VMs in the local virtual network will be allowed/disallowed in remote virtual network. Default is true.
Optional. If gateway links can be used in remote virtual networking to link to this virtual network. Default is false.
Optional. Whether the VMs in the local virtual network space would be able to access the VMs in remote virtual network space. Default is true.
Optional. Do not verify the provisioning state of the remote gateway. Default is true.

Optional. If remote gateways can be used on this virtual network. If the flag is set to true, and allowGatewayTransit on remote peering is also true, virtual network will use gateways of remote virtual network for transit. Only one peering can have this flag set to true. This flag cannot be set if virtual network already has a gateway. Default is false.
Required. The Name of the subnet resource.
Conditional. The address prefix for the subnet. Required if `addressPrefixes` is empty.
Conditional. List of address prefixes for the subnet. Required if `addressPrefix` is empty.
Required. The Resource ID of the IPAM pool.
Required. The Resource ID of the IPAM pool.
Required. Number of IP addresses allocated from the pool.
Conditional. The address space for the subnet, deployed from IPAM Pool. Required if `addressPrefixes` and `addressPrefix` is empty and the VNet address space configured to use IPAM Pool.
Optional. Application gateway IP configurations of virtual network resource.
Optional. The delegation to enable on the subnet.
Optional. The resource ID of the NAT Gateway to use for the subnet.
Optional. The resource ID of the network security group to assign to the subnet.
Optional. enable or disable apply network policies on private endpoint in the subnet.
Optional. enable or disable apply network policies on private link service in the subnet.
Optional. Array of role assignments to create.
Optional. The resource ID of the route table to assign to the subnet.
Optional. An array of service endpoint policies.
Optional. The service endpoints to enable on the subnet.

Optional. Set this property to false to disable default outbound connectivity for all VMs in the subnet. This property can only be set at the time of subnet creation and cannot be updated for an existing subnet.

Optional. Set this property to Tenant to allow sharing subnet with other subscriptions in your AAD tenant. This property can only be set if defaultOutboundAccess is set to false, both properties can only be set if subnet is empty.
Optional. The name of the diagnostic setting.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set the specific logs to collect here.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category group for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `allLogs` to collect all logs.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of logs that will be streamed. "allLogs" includes all possible logs for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable log collection.
Required. Name of a Diagnostic Metric category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `AllMetrics` to collect all metrics.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of metrics that will be streamed. "allMetrics" includes all possible metrics for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable metric collection.
Optional. A string indicating whether the export to Log Analytics should use the default destination type, i.e. AzureDiagnostics, or use a destination type.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic log analytics workspace. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic storage account. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic event hub authorization rule for the Event Hubs namespace in which the event hub should be created or streamed to.

Optional. Name of the diagnostic event hub within the namespace to which logs are streamed. Without this, an event hub is created for each log category. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. The full ARM resource ID of the Marketplace resource to which you would like to send Diagnostic Logs.
An AVM-aligned type for a diagnostic setting. To be used if both logs & metrics are supported by the resource provider.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Required. The role to assign. You can provide either the display name of the role definition, the role definition GUID, or its fully qualified ID in the following format: '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/c2f4ef07-c644-48eb-af81-4b1b4947fb11'.
Required. The principal ID of the principal (user/group/identity) to assign the role to.
Optional. The principal type of the assigned principal ID.
Optional. The description of the role assignment.

Optional. The conditions on the role assignment. This limits the resources it can be assigned to. e.g.: @Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:ContainerName] StringEqualsIgnoreCase "foo_storage_container".
Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
An AVM-aligned type for a role assignment.
Required. The name of the Virtual Network (vNet).
Optional. Location for all resources.

Required. An Array of 1 or more IP Address Prefixes OR the resource ID of the IPAM pool to be used for the Virtual Network. When specifying an IPAM pool resource ID you must also set a value for the parameter called `ipamPoolNumberOfIpAddresses`.
Optional. Number of IP addresses allocated from the pool. To be used only when the addressPrefix param is defined with a resource ID of an IPAM pool.
Optional. The BGP community associated with the virtual network.
Optional. An Array of subnets to deploy to the Virtual Network.
Optional. DNS Servers associated to the Virtual Network.

Optional. Resource ID of the DDoS protection plan to assign the VNET to. If it's left blank, DDoS protection will not be configured. If it's provided, the VNET created by this template will be attached to the referenced DDoS protection plan. The DDoS protection plan can exist in the same or in a different subscription.
Optional. Virtual Network Peering configurations.

Optional. Indicates if encryption is enabled on virtual network and if VM without encryption is allowed in encrypted VNet. Requires the EnableVNetEncryption feature to be registered for the subscription and a supported region to use this property.
Optional. If the encrypted VNet allows VM that does not support encryption. Can only be used when vnetEncryption is enabled.

Optional. The flow timeout in minutes for the Virtual Network, which is used to enable connection tracking for intra-VM flows. Possible values are between 4 and 30 minutes. Default value 0 will set the property to null.
Optional. The diagnostic settings of the service.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.
Optional. Array of role assignments to create.
Optional. Tags of the resource.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
Optional. Indicates if VM protection is enabled for all the subnets in the virtual network.

metadata.name: Virtual Network Subnets
metadata.description: This module deploys a Virtual Network Subnet.
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Optional. Enable or disable apply network policies on private link service in the subnet.
Conditional. List of address prefixes for the subnet. Required if `addressPrefix` is empty.

Optional. Set this property to false to disable default outbound connectivity for all VMs in the subnet. This property can only be set at the time of subnet creation and cannot be updated for an existing subnet.

Optional. Set this property to Tenant to allow sharing the subnet with other subscriptions in your AAD tenant. This property can only be set if defaultOutboundAccess is set to false, both properties can only be set if the subnet is empty.
Optional. Application gateway IP configurations of virtual network resource.
Optional. An array of service endpoint policies.
Optional. Array of role assignments to create.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
The resource group the virtual network peering was deployed into.
The name of the virtual network peering.
The resource ID of the virtual network peering.
The address prefix for the subnet.

metadata.name: Virtual Network Peerings
metadata.description: This module deploys a Virtual Network Peering.
Optional. The Name of VNET Peering resource. If not provided, default value will be localVnetName-remoteVnetName.
Conditional. The name of the parent Virtual Network to add the peering to. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Required. The Resource ID of the VNet that is this Local VNet is being peered to. Should be in the format of a Resource ID.
Optional. Whether the forwarded traffic from the VMs in the local virtual network will be allowed/disallowed in remote virtual network. Default is true.
Optional. If gateway links can be used in remote virtual networking to link to this virtual network. Default is false.
Optional. Whether the VMs in the local virtual network space would be able to access the VMs in remote virtual network space. Default is true.
Optional. If we need to verify the provisioning state of the remote gateway. Default is true.

If the flag is set to true, and allowGatewayTransit on remote peering is also true, virtual network will use gateways of remote virtual network for transit. Only one peering can have this flag set to true. This flag cannot be set if virtual network already has a gateway. Default is false.
The resource group the virtual network peering was deployed into.
The name of the virtual network peering.
The resource ID of the virtual network peering.

Required. Specifies the size for the VMs.

Optional. This property can be used by user in the request to enable or disable the Host Encryption for the virtual machine. This will enable the encryption for all the disks including Resource/Temp disk at host itself. For security reasons, it is recommended to set encryptionAtHost to True. Restrictions: Cannot be enabled if Azure Disk Encryption (guest-VM encryption using bitlocker/DM-Crypt) is enabled on your VMs.

Optional. Specifies the SecurityType of the virtual machine. It has to be set to any specified value to enable UefiSettings. The default behavior is: UefiSettings will not be enabled unless this property is set.
Optional. Specifies whether secure boot should be enabled on the virtual machine. This parameter is part of the UefiSettings. SecurityType should be set to TrustedLaunch to enable UefiSettings.
Optional. Specifies whether vTPM should be enabled on the virtual machine. This parameter is part of the UefiSettings.  SecurityType should be set to TrustedLaunch to enable UefiSettings.
Required. OS image reference. In case of marketplace images, it's the combination of the publisher, offer, sku, version attributes. In case of custom images it's the resource ID of the custom image.

Required. Specifies the OS disk. For security reasons, it is recommended to specify DiskEncryptionSet into the osDisk object.  Restrictions: DiskEncryptionSet cannot be enabled if Azure Disk Encryption (guest-VM encryption using bitlocker/DM-Crypt) is enabled on your VMs.

Optional. Specifies the data disks. For security reasons, it is recommended to specify DiskEncryptionSet into the dataDisk object. Restrictions: DiskEncryptionSet cannot be enabled if Azure Disk Encryption (guest-VM encryption using bitlocker/DM-Crypt) is enabled on your VMs.

Optional. The flag that enables or disables a capability to have one or more managed data disks with UltraSSD_LRS storage account type on the VM or VMSS. Managed disks with storage account type UltraSSD_LRS can be added to a virtual machine or virtual machine scale set only if this property is enabled.
Optional. The flag that enables or disables hibernation capability on the VM.
Required. Administrator username.
Optional. When specifying a Windows Virtual Machine, this value should be passed.
Optional. UserData for the VM, which must be base-64 encoded. Customer should not pass any secrets in here.
Optional. Custom data associated to the VM, this value will be automatically converted into base64 to account for the expected VM format.
Optional. Specifies set of certificates that should be installed onto the virtual machine.
Optional. Specifies the priority for the virtual machine.

Optional. Resource ID of an availability set. Cannot be used in combination with availability zone nor scale set.
Optional. Specifies the gallery applications that should be made available to the VM/VMSS.

Required. If set to 1, 2 or 3, the availability zone for all VMs is hardcoded to that value. If zero, then availability zones is not used. Cannot be used in combination with availability set nor scale set.
Required. Configures NICs and PIPs.
Optional. Recovery service vault name to add VMs to backup.
Optional. Resource group of the backup recovery service vault. If not provided the current resource group name is considered by default.
Optional. Backup policy the VMs should be using for backup. If not provided, it will use the DefaultPolicy from the backup recovery service vault.
Optional. The configuration for auto-shutdown.
Optional. The resource Id of a maintenance configuration for this VM.
Optional. Specifies whether extension operations should be allowed on the virtual machine. This may only be set to False when no extensions are present on the virtual machine.
Optional. Required if name is specified. Password of the user specified in user parameter.
Optional. The configuration for the [Domain Join] extension. Must at least contain the ["enabled": true] property to be executed.

Optional. The configuration for the [Anti Malware] extension. Must at least contain the ["enabled": true] property to be executed.
Optional. The configuration for the [Monitoring Agent] extension. Must at least contain the ["enabled": true] property to be executed.
Optional. The configuration for the [Dependency Agent] extension. Must at least contain the ["enabled": true] property to be executed.

Optional. The configuration for the [Desired State Configuration] extension. Must at least contain the ["enabled": true] property to be executed.
Optional. The configuration for the [Custom Script] extension. Must at least contain the ["enabled": true] property to be executed.

Optional. The configuration for the [Guest Configuration] extension. Must at least contain the ["enabled": true] property to be executed. Needs a managed identy.

Optional. An object that contains the extension specific protected settings.

Generated. Do not provide a value! This date value is used to generate a registration token.

Optional. SAS token validity length to use to download files from storage accounts. Usage: 'PT8H' - valid for 8 hours; 'P5D' - valid for 5 days; 'P1Y' - valid for 1 year. When not provided, the SAS token will be valid for 8 hours.
Required. The chosen OS type.
Optional. Specifies whether password authentication should be disabled.

Optional. Indicates whether virtual machine agent should be provisioned on the virtual machine. When this property is not specified in the request body, default behavior is to set it to true. This will ensure that VM Agent is installed on the VM so that extensions can be added to the VM later.

Optional. Indicates whether Automatic Updates is enabled for the Windows virtual machine. Default value is true. When patchMode is set to Manual, this parameter must be set to false. For virtual machine scale sets, this property can be updated and updates will take effect on OS reprovisioning.

Optional. VM guest patching orchestration mode. 'AutomaticByOS' & 'Manual' are for Windows only, 'ImageDefault' for Linux only. Refer to 'https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/automatic-vm-guest-patching'.
Optional. Enables customer to schedule patching without accidental upgrades.
Optional. Specifies the reboot setting for all AutomaticByPlatform patch installation operations.
Optional. VM guest patching assessment mode. Set it to 'AutomaticByPlatform' to enable automatically check for updates every 24 hours.

Optional. Enables customers to patch their Azure VMs without requiring a reboot. For enableHotpatching, the 'provisionVMAgent' must be set to true and 'patchMode' must be set to 'AutomaticByPlatform'.
Optional. Specifies the time zone of the virtual machine. e.g. 'Pacific Standard Time'. Possible values can be `TimeZoneInfo.id` value from time zones returned by `TimeZoneInfo.GetSystemTimeZones`.

Optional. Specifies additional XML formatted information that can be included in the Unattend.xml file, which is used by Windows Setup. Contents are defined by setting name, component name, and the pass in which the content is applied.
Optional. Specifies the Windows Remote Management listeners. This enables remote Windows PowerShell.

Optional. The configuration profile of automanage. Either '/providers/Microsoft.Automanage/bestPractices/AzureBestPracticesProduction', 'providers/Microsoft.Automanage/bestPractices/AzureBestPracticesDevTest' or the resource Id of custom profile.

metadata.name: Public IP Addresses
metadata.description: This module deploys a Public IP Address.

Required. The domain name label. The concatenation of the domain name label and the regionalized DNS zone make up the fully qualified domain name associated with the public IP address. If a domain name label is specified, an A DNS record is created for the public IP in the Microsoft Azure DNS system.

Optional. The domain name label scope. If a domain name label and a domain name label scope are specified, an A DNS record is created for the public IP in the Microsoft Azure DNS system with a hashed value includes in FQDN.
Optional. The Fully Qualified Domain Name of the A DNS record associated with the public IP. This is the concatenation of the domainNameLabel and the regionalized DNS zone.

Optional. The reverse FQDN. A user-visible, fully qualified domain name that resolves to this public IP address. If the reverseFqdn is specified, then a PTR DNS record is created pointing from the IP address in the in-addr.arpa domain to the reverse FQDN.
Required. The resource ID of the DDOS protection plan associated with the public IP address.
Optional. The DDoS protection plan associated with the public IP address.
Required. The DDoS protection policy customizations.
Required. The IP tag type.
Required. The IP tag.
Optional. The name of the diagnostic setting.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set the specific logs to collect here.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category group for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `allLogs` to collect all logs.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of logs that will be streamed. "allLogs" includes all possible logs for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable log collection.
Required. Name of a Diagnostic Metric category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `AllMetrics` to collect all metrics.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of metrics that will be streamed. "allMetrics" includes all possible metrics for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable metric collection.
Optional. A string indicating whether the export to Log Analytics should use the default destination type, i.e. AzureDiagnostics, or use a destination type.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic log analytics workspace. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic storage account. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic event hub authorization rule for the Event Hubs namespace in which the event hub should be created or streamed to.

Optional. Name of the diagnostic event hub within the namespace to which logs are streamed. Without this, an event hub is created for each log category. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. The full ARM resource ID of the Marketplace resource to which you would like to send Diagnostic Logs.
An AVM-aligned type for a diagnostic setting. To be used if both logs & metrics are supported by the resource provider.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Required. The principal ID of the principal (user/group/identity) to assign the role to.
Optional. The principal type of the assigned principal ID.
Optional. The description of the role assignment.

Optional. The conditions on the role assignment. This limits the resources it can be assigned to. e.g.: @Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:ContainerName] StringEqualsIgnoreCase "foo_storage_container".
Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
An AVM-aligned type for a role assignment.
Required. The name of the Public IP Address.
Optional. Resource ID of the Public IP Prefix object. This is only needed if you want your Public IPs created in a PIP Prefix.
Optional. The public IP address allocation method.
Optional. A list of availability zones denoting the IP allocated for the resource needs to come from.
Optional. IP address version.
Optional. The DNS settings of the public IP address.
Optional. The list of tags associated with the public IP address.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.

Optional. Tier of a public IP address SKU.

metadata.name: Virtual Machine Extensions

Required. The name of the extension handler publisher.
Required. Specifies the type of the extension; an example is "CustomScriptExtension".
Required. Specifies the version of the script handler.

Required. Indicates whether the extension should use a newer minor version if one is available at deployment time. Once deployed, however, the extension will not upgrade minor versions unless redeployed, even with this property set to true.
Optional. How the extension handler should be forced to update even if the extension configuration has not changed.
Optional. Any object that contains the extension specific settings.
Optional. Any object that contains the extension specific protected settings.

Optional. Tags of the resource.
The name of the extension.
The resource ID of the extension.
The name of the Resource Group the extension was created in.
The location the resource was deployed into.

Required. The name of the Azure Cognitive Search service to create or update. Search service names must only contain lowercase letters, digits or dashes, cannot use dash as the first two or last one characters, cannot contain consecutive dashes, and must be between 2 and 60 characters in length. Search service names must be globally unique since they are part of the service URI (https://<name>.search.windows.net). You cannot change the service name after the service is created.
Optional. Defines the options for how the data plane API of a Search service authenticates requests. Must remain an empty object {} if 'disableLocalAuth' is set to true.

Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
Optional. Describes a policy that determines how resources within the search service are to be encrypted with Customer Managed Keys.

You can set this property to enable up to 3 high density partitions that allow up to 1000 indexes, which is much higher than the maximum indexes allowed for any other SKU. For the standard3 SKU, the value is either 'default' or 'highDensity'. For all other SKUs, this value must be 'default'.
Optional. Location for all Resources.
Optional. The lock settings for all Resources in the solution.
Optional. Network specific rules that determine how the Azure Cognitive Search service may be reached.

Optional. Configuration details for private endpoints. For security reasons, it is recommended to use private endpoints whenever possible.

Optional. Defines the SKU of an Azure Cognitive Search Service, which determines price tier and capacity limits.
Optional. The managed identity definition for this resource.
Optional. The diagnostic settings of the service.
Optional. Tags to help categorize the resource in the Azure portal.

Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of logs that will be streamed. "allLogs" includes all possible logs for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable log collection.

Optional. The name of metrics that will be streamed. "allMetrics" includes all possible metrics for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable metric collection.

Optional. The immutability period for the blobs in the container since the policy creation, in days.

Optional. This property can only be changed for unlocked time-based retention policies. When enabled, new blocks can be written to an append blob while maintaining immutability protection and compliance. Only new blocks can be added and any existing blocks cannot be modified or deleted. This property cannot be changed with ExtendImmutabilityPolicy API.

Optional. This property can only be changed for unlocked time-based retention policies. When enabled, new blocks can be written to both "Append and Block Blobs" while maintaining immutability protection and compliance. Only new blocks can be added and any existing blocks cannot be modified or deleted. This property cannot be changed with ExtendImmutabilityPolicy API. The "allowProtectedAppendWrites" and "allowProtectedAppendWritesAll" properties are mutually exclusive.
The type for an immutability policy.
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Optional. The description of the role assignment.

Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
An AVM-aligned type for a role assignment.
Conditional. The name of the parent Storage Account. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Optional. Automatic Snapshot is enabled if set to true.
Optional. The blob service properties for change feed events. Indicates whether change feed event logging is enabled for the Blob service.

Optional. Indicates whether change feed event logging is enabled for the Blob service. Indicates the duration of changeFeed retention in days. If left blank, it indicates an infinite retention of the change feed.
Optional. The blob service properties for container soft delete. Indicates whether DeleteRetentionPolicy is enabled.
Optional. Indicates the number of days that the deleted item should be retained.

Optional. This property when set to true allows deletion of the soft deleted blob versions and snapshots. This property cannot be used with blob restore policy. This property only applies to blob service and does not apply to containers or file share.
Optional. The List of CORS rules. You can include up to five CorsRule elements in the request.

Optional. Indicates the default version to use for requests to the Blob service if an incoming request's version is not specified. Possible values include version 2008-10-27 and all more recent versions.
Optional. The blob service properties for blob soft delete.
Optional. Indicates the number of days that the deleted blob should be retained.

Optional. This property when set to true allows deletion of the soft deleted blob versions and snapshots. This property cannot be used with blob restore policy. This property only applies to blob service and does not apply to containers or file share.
Optional. Use versioning to automatically maintain previous versions of your blobs. Cannot be enabled for ADLS Gen2 storage accounts.
Optional. The blob service property to configure last access time based tracking policy. When set to true last access time based tracking is enabled.
Optional. The blob service properties for blob restore policy. If point-in-time restore is enabled, then versioning, change feed, and blob soft delete must also be enabled.
Optional. How long this blob can be restored. It should be less than DeleteRetentionPolicy days.
Optional. Blob containers to create.
Optional. The diagnostic settings of the service.

Optional. Flag to indicate whether "Free Tier" is enabled. Defaults to false.
Optional. Enables the account to write in multiple locations. Periodic backup must be used if enabled. Defaults to false.
Optional. Disable write operations on metadata resources (databases, containers, throughput) via account keys. Defaults to true.
Optional. The maximum stale requests. Required for "BoundedStaleness" consistency level. Valid ranges, Single Region: 10 to 1000000. Multi Region: 100000 to 1000000. Defaults to 100000.
Optional. The maximum lag time in minutes. Required for "BoundedStaleness" consistency level. Valid ranges, Single Region: 5 to 84600. Multi Region: 300 to 86400. Defaults to 300.
Optional. Specifies the MongoDB server version to use if using Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB RU. Defaults to "4.2".
Optional. Configuration for databases when using Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL.
Optional. Configuration for databases when using Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB RU.
Optional. Configuration for databases when using Azure Cosmos DB for Apache Gremlin.
Optional. Configuration for databases when using Azure Cosmos DB for Table.
Optional. Configuration for keyspaces when using Azure Cosmos DB for Apache Cassandra.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
Optional. The total throughput limit imposed on this account in request units per second (RU/s). Default to unlimited throughput.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.
Optional. An array of control plane Azure role-based access control assignments.
Optional. Configurations for Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL native role-based access control definitions. Allows the creations of custom role definitions.
Optional. Configurations for Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL native role-based access control assignments.
Optional. Configurations for Azure Cosmos DB for Apache Cassandra native role-based access control definitions. Allows the creations of custom role definitions.
Optional. Azure Cosmos DB for Apache Cassandra native data plane role-based access control assignments. Each assignment references a role definition unique identifier and a principal identifier.
Optional. The diagnostic settings for the service.
Optional. A list of Azure Cosmos DB specific capabilities for the account.
Optional. Configures the backup mode. Periodic backup must be used if multiple write locations are used. Defaults to "Continuous".
Optional. Configuration values to specify the retention period for continuous mode backup. Default to "Continuous30Days".
Optional. An integer representing the interval in minutes between two backups. This setting only applies to the periodic backup type. Defaults to 240.
Optional. An integer representing the time (in hours) that each backup is retained. This setting only applies to the periodic backup type. Defaults to 8.
Optional. Setting that indicates the type of backup residency. This setting only applies to the periodic backup type. Defaults to "Local".
Optional. Configuration details for private endpoints. For security reasons, it is advised to use private endpoints whenever possible.
Optional. The network configuration of this module. Defaults to `{ ipRules: [], virtualNetworkRules: [], publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' }`.
Optional. Setting that indicates the minimum allowed TLS version. Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB RU and Apache Cassandra only work with TLS 1.2 or later. Defaults to "Tls12" (TLS 1.2).
Optional. Flag to indicate enabling/disabling of Burst Capacity feature on the account. Cannot be enabled for serverless accounts.
Optional. Enables the cassandra connector on the Cosmos DB C* account.
Optional. Flag to enable/disable the 'Partition Merge' feature on the account.
Optional. Flag to enable/disable the 'PerRegionPerPartitionAutoscale' feature on the account.
Optional. Analytical storage specific properties.
Optional. The CORS policy for the Cosmos DB database account.

Optional. The default identity for accessing key vault used in features like customer managed keys. Use `FirstPartyIdentity` to use the tenant-level CosmosDB enterprise application. The default identity needs to be explicitly set by the users.

metadata.name: DocumentDB Database Account SQL Databases
metadata.description: This module deploys a SQL Database in a CosmosDB Account.
Required. Name of the container.

Optional. Default to 0. Indicates how long data should be retained in the analytical store, for a container. Analytical store is enabled when ATTL is set with a value other than 0. If the value is set to -1, the analytical store retains all historical data, irrespective of the retention of the data in the transactional store.
Optional. The conflict resolution policy for the container. Conflicts and conflict resolution policies are applicable if the Azure Cosmos DB account is configured with multiple write regions.

Optional. Default to -1. Default time to live (in seconds). With Time to Live or TTL, Azure Cosmos DB provides the ability to delete items automatically from a container after a certain time period. If the value is set to "-1", it is equal to infinity, and items don't expire by default.

Optional. Default to 400. Request Units per second. Will be ignored if autoscaleSettingsMaxThroughput is used. For best performance for large production workloads, it is recommended to set dedicated throughput (autoscale or manual) at the container level and not at the database level.

Optional. Specifies the Autoscale settings and represents maximum throughput, the resource can scale up to. The autoscale throughput should have valid throughput values between 1000 and 1000000 inclusive in increments of 1000. If value is set to null, then autoscale will be disabled. For best performance for large production workloads, it is recommended to set dedicated throughput (autoscale or manual) at the container level and not at the database level.
Optional. Tags of the SQL Database resource.
Required. List of paths using which data within the container can be partitioned. For kind=MultiHash it can be up to 3. For anything else it needs to be exactly 1.
Optional. Indexing policy of the container.
Optional. The unique key policy configuration containing a list of unique keys that enforces uniqueness constraint on documents in the collection in the Azure Cosmos DB service.
Optional. Default to Hash. Indicates the kind of algorithm used for partitioning.
Optional. Default to 1 for Hash and 2 for MultiHash - 1 is not allowed for MultiHash. Version of the partition key definition.
The type of a container.
Conditional. The name of the parent Database Account. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Required. Name of the SQL database .
Optional. Array of containers to deploy in the SQL database.

Optional. Request units per second. Will be ignored if autoscaleSettingsMaxThroughput is used. Setting throughput at the database level is only recommended for development/test or when workload across all containers in the shared throughput database is uniform. For best performance for large production workloads, it is recommended to set dedicated throughput (autoscale or manual) at the container level and not at the database level.

Optional. Specifies the Autoscale settings and represents maximum throughput, the resource can scale up to. The autoscale throughput should have valid throughput values between 1000 and 1000000 inclusive in increments of 1000. If value is set to null, then autoscale will be disabled. Setting throughput at the database level is only recommended for development/test or when workload across all containers in the shared throughput database is uniform. For best performance for large production workloads, it is recommended to set dedicated throughput (autoscale or manual) at the container level and not at the database level.
Optional. Tags of the SQL database resource.

Optional. Default to 400. Request Units per second. Will be ignored if autoscaleSettingsMaxThroughput is used. For best performance for large production workloads, it is recommended to set dedicated throughput (autoscale or manual) at the container level and not at the database level.

metadata.name: DocumentDB Database Account SQL Role Definitions.
metadata.description: This module deploys a SQL Role Definision in a CosmosDB Account.

Required. An array of data actions that are allowed.

Optional. A set of fully qualified Scopes at or below which Role Assignments may be created using this Role Definition. This will allow application of this Role Definition on the entire database account or any underlying Database / Collection. Must have at least one element. Scopes higher than Database account are not enforceable as assignable Scopes. Note that resources referenced in assignable Scopes need not exist. Defaults to the current account.
Optional. An array of SQL Role Assignments to be created for the SQL Role Definition.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.

metadata.name: DocumentDB Database Account Cassandra Role Definitions.
metadata.description: This module deploys a Cassandra Role Definition in a CosmosDB Account.
Optional. The unique identifier of the role assignment.
Required. The unique identifier for the associated AAD principal.
Optional. The data plane resource path for which access is being granted. Defaults to the current account.
Conditional. The name of the parent Database Account. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Optional. The unique identifier of the Role Definition.
Required. A user-friendly name for the Role Definition. Must be unique for the database account.

Optional. An array of data actions that are allowed. Note: Valid data action strings for Cassandra API are currently undocumented (as of API version 2025-05-01-preview). Please refer to official Azure documentation once available.

Optional. An array of data actions that are denied. Note: Unlike SQL RBAC, Cassandra RBAC supports deny rules (notDataActions) for granular access control. Valid data action strings are currently undocumented (as of API version 2025-05-01-preview).

Optional. A set of fully qualified Scopes at or below which Role Assignments may be created using this Role Definition. This will allow application of this Role Definition on the entire database account or any underlying Database / Keyspace. Must have at least one element. Scopes higher than Database account are not enforceable as assignable Scopes. Note that resources referenced in assignable Scopes need not exist. Defaults to the current account.
Optional. An array of Cassandra Role Assignments to be created for the Cassandra Role Definition.

Setting throughput at the keyspace level is only recommended for development/test or when workload across all tables in the shared throughput keyspace is uniform. properties.condition:
[replace('      (
        (
          !(ActionMatches{''Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers/blobs/tags/read''})
          AND  !(ActionMatches{''Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers/blobs/filter/action''})
          AND  !(ActionMatches{''Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers/blobs/tags/write''})
        )
        OR
        (@Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:name] StringStartsWithIgnoreCase ''#projectWorkspaceId#''
        AND @Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:name] StringLikeIgnoreCase ''*-azureml-agent'')
      )
      ', '#projectWorkspaceId#', parameters('projectWorkspaceId'))]

Required. The failover priority of the region. A failover priority of 0 indicates a write region. The maximum value for a failover priority = (total number of regions - 1). Failover priority values must be unique for each of the regions in which the database account exists.
Optional. Flag to indicate whether or not this region is an AvailabilityZone region. Defaults to true.

The type for the failover location.
Optional. The unique name of the role assignment.

Optional. The unique identifier of the role-based access control definition.

Optional. An array of data actions that are allowed.

Optional. A set of fully-qualified scopes at or below which role-based access control assignments may be created using this definition. This setting allows application of this definition on the entire account or any underlying resource. This setting must have at least one element. Scopes higher than the account level are not enforceable as assignable scopes. Resources referenced in assignable scopes do not need to exist at creation. Defaults to the current account scope.
Optional. An array of role-based access control assignments to be created for the definition.
The type for an Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL or Table native role-based access control definition.
Required. Name of the database .

Optional. Request units per second. Will be ignored if `autoscaleSettingsMaxThroughput` is used. Setting throughput at the database level is only recommended for development/test or when workload across all containers in the shared throughput database is uniform. For best performance for large production workloads, it is recommended to set dedicated throughput (autoscale or manual) at the container level and not at the database level. Defaults to 400.

Optional. Specifies the autoscale settings and represents maximum throughput the resource can scale up to. The autoscale throughput should have valid throughput values between 1000 and 1000000 inclusive in increments of 1000. If the value is not set, then autoscale will be disabled. Setting throughput at the database level is only recommended for development/test or when workload across all containers in the shared throughput database is uniform. For best performance for large production workloads, it is recommended to set dedicated throughput (autoscale or manual) at the container level and not at the database level.
Required. Name of the container.
Required. List of paths using which data within the container can be partitioned. For kind=MultiHash it can be up to 3. For anything else it needs to be exactly 1.

Optional. Default to 0. Indicates how long data should be retained in the analytical store, for a container. Analytical store is enabled when ATTL is set with a value other than 0. If the value is set to -1, the analytical store retains all historical data, irrespective of the retention of the data in the transactional store.

Optional. Specifies the Autoscale settings and represents maximum throughput, the resource can scale up to. The autoscale throughput should have valid throughput values between 1000 and 1000000 inclusive in increments of 1000. If value is set to null, then autoscale will be disabled. For best performance for large production workloads, it is recommended to set dedicated throughput (autoscale or manual) at the container level.
